mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness signals robust consumer spending capacity, driving corporate earnings growth and lifting S&P 500 prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit expansion and household wealth effects, which jointly support equity valuations over a 60-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: During early recovery phases, accelerating industrial production growth triggers rapid upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth over 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all post-recession episodes since 1980.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices exceeding a 0.4 percent month-over-month increase trigger expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and reduces S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent and monthly payroll gains exceeding 150,000 confirm robust aggregate demand, which supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and PAYEMS signal produces negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of qualifying periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll expansion reduces recession risk premiums embedded in equity valuations, steadily lifting S&P 500 prices over a 60-day trading window.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns show no statistically significant correlation with PAYEMS growth rates over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial output exceeding 1.0 percent over three consecutive months signals weakening manufacturing demand, prompting immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO falls below the 3-month threshold.
